ranked_scenarios.summary: [Layout Expt] Make card width consistent across all breakpoints

description.text:
Created attachment 9420005
Example "non-narrow" card on small break point.

Currently, the card width will get more narrow (~216px) on smaller breakpoints. The new layouts keep it consistent across the board at `296px`. 

Links: 
- [Figma Designs](https://www.figma.com/design/zhG60dfgG1ovYnRIGbPQYr/Layouts?node-id=276-59989&t=M8x0hOYiZx31P0SL-4) 
- [Breakdown Document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ymPRBseT4MJXE0HP2tx_OFXpC_x19PzUr7WTJ50hgpE/edit)

ranked_scenarios.summary: [Layout Expt] Add fourth column (Recommended stories) on super-wide screens

description.text:
Created attachment 9420000
Fourth column to Recommended Stories section

Applies to both variants: 

Links: 
- [Figma Designs](https://www.figma.com/design/zhG60dfgG1ovYnRIGbPQYr/Layouts?node-id=276-63692&t=M8x0hOYiZx31P0SL-4) 
- [Breakdown Document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ymPRBseT4MJXE0HP2tx_OFXpC_x19PzUr7WTJ50hgpE/edit)

We want to keep our dependencies up to date as much as possible. 
Moving to webpack 5 seems like an easy win for our setup.
